goal: One shared cart for a household — additions merge instead of colliding.

screens.name: Household Setup
screens.purpose: Create a new household or join an existing one via code/link.

states.empty: No household yet — two clear CTAs: 'Create household' / 'Join with code'.
states.success: Household created or joined -> routes to shared_cart.

screens.name: Invite / Share
screens.purpose: Generate and share the join code or link.

states.success: QR code + copyable link + native share sheet.

screens.name: Shared Cart
screens.purpose: Main real-time view — everyone's added items in one list.

states.empty: 'Cart's empty — add something' with quick-add suggestions.
states.success: Each row tagged with addedBy avatar + timestamp; new items animate in as they arrive from other members.
states.error: Listener disconnected -> subtle 'Reconnecting...' banner, cart stays usable offline-first, syncs on reconnect.

screens.name: Cart Item Detail
screens.purpose: Claim an item (so no one else duplicates it) or remove it.

states.success: Claimed state shows 'Claimed by [name]' badge, others see it greyed to discourage duplicate buys.

userFlow: User creates a household during onboarding, gets a join code.
Shares code with roommates via invite_share.
Roommate joins -> both land on the same shared_cart in real time.
User adds detergent; roommate adds eggs from their own phone -> both appear instantly, tagged with who added them.
Roommate claims 'detergent' before a store run so no one else buys it twice.
edgeCases: Someone removes an item another person added -> soft toast notification, not a silent delete.
A member leaves the household -> their previously-added items stay in the cart, ownership label just shows their name (not orphaned/blank).
Two people claim the same item within the same second -> first write wins, second gets a toast explaining it's already claimed.
Invite link expired/reused -> clear error, not a silent failure to join.
notes: Reuses the same membership + role pattern already built for AATCe Connect's 5-role system. The shared_cart schema here is intentionally identical in shape to Group Ordering's participant cart (see 05_group_ordering.json) so the two features can share implementation.
